mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the Industrial Production Index signals stronger manufacturing activity that boosts near-term earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% imposes a restrictive cost of capital that reduces corporate investment and equity risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.0% produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the strategy's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in industrial production signal a broad contraction in economic activity that triggers recession risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change negative and INDPRO 1-month change negative
failure_condition: Joint declines in PAYEMS and INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of concurrent instances, or the interaction term exhibits a t-statistic below 1.5 in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns exclusively in post-recession recovery periods because residential construction leads the business cycle when monetary policy is accommodative and pent-up demand is released.
failure_condition: Positive 3-month changes in HOUST during post-recession recovery periods are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals an inflation surprise that raises discount rate expectations and compresses real earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL 1-month change > 0.3% produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 3.8% signals an overheated labor market that increases the probability of additional Federal Reserve tightening, raising the equity risk premium and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE < 3.8% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's information coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days because macroeconomic momentum is rapidly priced into equity markets during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1 during early expansion are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail sales indicates sustained consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
